cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking for the purpose of the author's "loud thank you" to the doorman. The options are A through D. I need to analyze the text to determine which is correct. First, let me recap the story. The author and her family are on their way to California and stop for lunch. At the restaurant, a man (referred to as the doorman) opens the door for them and greets them. The kids comment on his smell, and the author tells them to look beyond the dirt. She feels upset by their rudeness and wants to be a good example. Later, when she goes back to get car-sick pills, the doorman is opening the door again, and she says a loud thank you. Then she buys him food, explaining to her family, and the doorman becomes very grateful. The key part here is why the author said a loud "thank you" when she was going out to get the pills. Let's look at the text around that moment. It says: "When I returned, we talked a bit. He said he was not allowed inside unless he bought food."
